# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder_14b)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template for a complete GCP application stack (Compute Engine, Cloud SQL, Cloud Storage, Load Balancer). Focus on core infrastructure. Skip Cloud Logging and Monitoring configuration to reduce complexity in our initial MVP deployment.
# Category: cloud_monitoring

resources:
  - name: gce-vm-instance
    type: compute.v1.instance
    properties:
      zone: us-central1-a
      machineType: f1-micro
      disks:
        - deviceName: boot-disk
          type: PERSISTENT
          boot: true
          autoDelete: true
          initializeParams:
            sourceImage: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-9
      networkInterfaces:
        - network: global/networks/default
  - name: cloud-sql-instance
    type: sqladmin.v1beta4.instance
    properties:
      databaseVersion: MYSQL_5_7
      settings:
        tier: db-f1-micro
      region: us-central1
  - name: cloud-storage-bucket
    type: storage.v1.bucket
    properties:
      locationType: REGIONAL
      location: US-CENTRAL1
  - name: load-balancer-forwarding-rule
    type: compute.v1.forwardingRule
    properties:
      IPProtocol: TCP
      portRange: 80-80
      target: $(ref.gce-vm-instance.selfLink)
  - name: load-balancer-target-pool
    type: compute.v1.targetPool
    properties:
      region: us-central1
      healthChecks:
        - $(ref.load-balancer-health-check.selfLink)
  - name: load-balancer-health-check
    type: compute.v1.httpHealthCheck
    properties:
      requestPath: /
